meta.perChapterInput: first 3 beats + last 3 beats (text+summary), beats sorted by beat_idx

Will the companions survive the perilous Trollmoors and the ancient threat stirring beneath?
Will the companions survive the Evermoors and escape the trolls?

directional_assessment: Across all 3 IWD books, partial-resolution is the dominant chapter dramatic-question shape (54.3% aggregate; 44.1% / 62.1% / 58.6% per book — modal in all three). Closed (full chapter resolution) is a strong but minority second at 18.5% aggregate. Deferred (cliffhanger on the SAME opening question) is rare (8.7%); replaced (opening question was a feint) and compound (multiple co-equal questions) sit near 10% and 7.6%. Cross-book ordering is stable: partial is modal in every book; closed is #2 in two of three books (halflings_gem swaps deferred for compound at #2-tier). Confidence floor 0.80 (median 0.90), n=92 (34 / 29 / 29). Directional verdict: the published Salvatore corpus is dominated by chapters that pose a focused dramatic question and resolve PART of it within the chapter while explicitly carrying a thread forward — a planner prior of "end most chapters with a partial resolution" matches the corpus, with closed and replaced as legitimate but minority shapes. The harness chapter-outline prompt (currently "end each non-final chapter purpose with a forward hook") under-specifies SHAPE: ~55% of the corpus partial-resolves, only ~9% truly defers (cliffhanger). Recommended target prompt addition: instruct the planner that ~55% of non-final chapters should be "partial-resolution" (the chapter answers part of the dramatic question and carries a thread forward), ~20% "closed" (full resolution within chapter), and the remaining ~25% spread across deferred/replaced/compound — with explicit guidance that compound and replaced are deliberate structural choices, not accidents.
